issues.claim: atma-aupamyena rendered as 'by analogy with the self / by the same standard one would apply to oneself'
issues.objection: The rendering is faithful to the operational reading. The metaphysical reading (similarity-of-selves) is named but not load-bearing in the engineering analog — acceptable scope choice.

notes: BG 6.32's engineering layer preserves atma-aupamyena verbatim, names both classical readings distinctly, and does not conflate. The Sanskrit fragment sukham va yadi va duhkham is quoted in the scenario. The verse's load-bearing terms (atma-aupamyena, samam pasyati, paramo) are preserved untranslated. PASS.
